recommendations: add a minimal smoke test suite for generated MCP endpoints (import + one call per endpoint group)
define explicit endpoint schemas with Pydantic models (including ndarray/list coercion and shape validation)
remove duplicate/case-variant endpoint aliases (e.g.
FitnessFunc vs fitnessfunc) via a single naming convention
add robust error translation in adapter/service layers (ValueError/TypeError/network errors → structured MCP errors)
gate dataset-fetch endpoints behind optional network flags and clear offline fallbacks
pin and document compatible dependency ranges in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` (including astroML/scikit-learn/astropy compatibility)
add CI workflow for MCP plugin (lint
type-check
unit tests
packaging check)
introduce caching/timeouts/retry controls for remote dataset functions
improve README_MCP with per-endpoint examples and parameter contracts
add observability hooks (request timing
endpoint-level success/failure counts
debug logging toggles)
enforce static typing across plugin files and run mypy/pyright
add contract tests to ensure generated endpoint signatures stay in sync with upstream astroML APIs
add input-size guards for heavy computations (periodograms/bayesian blocks) to prevent pathological runtime
provide a versioned manifest of exposed endpoints for backward-compatibility tracking
add security hardening (dependency audit + safe handling for file/network paths)
include benchmark baselines for key endpoints to catch regressions

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy (confidence 0.95, import feasibility 0.94) fits astroML’s Python package structure well.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and MCP plugin tests reported passed.
Repository has clear modular organization (classification, clustering, datasets, density_estimation, time_series, stats), enabling systematic endpoint extraction.
Low intrusiveness risk reduced integration friction with upstream code.
failure_reasons: Original project test result is marked failed despite 100% coverage metadata and no details, indicating test orchestration/reporting inconsistency.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architectural/context enrichment.
Quality telemetry is incomplete (0 for LOC, file size, tool endpoint count, and performance metrics), limiting evidence-based validation.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; effective for reliability but may be slower/less metadata-rich than direct VCS clone.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with medium complexity assessment and broad module detection; quality is acceptable despite DeepWiki failure.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed and produced expected MCP scaffolding files; endpoint list indicates meaningful exposure of astroML capabilities.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health checks passed; however, absence of detailed endpoint-level smoke/contract tests leaves functional depth unverified.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient data (reported 0 MB likely placeholder); cannot confirm efficiency.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient data (reported 0% likely placeholder); no reliable CPU profile available.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Likely low-to-moderate based on 7 generated files, but generated size reported as 0 indicates missing instrumentation.
